title: Twelfth Night by William Shakespeare | Analysis in Hindi
description:
Few comedies end with as much unease as Twelfth Night — Shakespeare leaves Malvolio humiliated and vowing revenge, Antonio unrewarded, and Olivia married to a stranger she barely knows, all while Viola spends her final moments still dressed as a man. For BA English Honours students at DU, SOL, or IGNOU, this is exactly why the ending of Twelfth Night problematizes the comic resolution — the laughter never fully drowns out the loneliness beneath it. Shakespeare's masterpiece rewards those who read against its festive surface, and that is precisely what this analysis in Hindi unpacks.
